item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really think that's the most underrated SCP. I loved everything about it. Obvious +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 from me, it's really good!

Also, my theory is that this entity is giving the Foundation the mnestics because she knows that the Escapee is coming, and this is the only way she can indirectly fight it. "But why would she care", you might ask. Remember: She's sapient and sentient, and while SCP-3125's document doesn't address its effects on non-human entities, I assume it would affect her the same way as us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m so happy you think so! Very much appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have no idea how happy this makes me to hear. I’m so glad I was able to do your idea justice in this article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-5997 is a sapient essophysical entity

You broke me. I'm immensely thankful that my concept of essophysics could be used in an article as well-written as this, and I apologize that I wasn't able to read it during the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was really well put together! I love it when scps have an initial file and keep adding on to the original file. It builds up this sense of mystery, and considering what series 6 is all about, that was an excellent choice! The entity itself was very interesting too, I like how we know what it is, but not why it does the things it does. All in all, very well done, you earned an upvote from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s great to hear, thank you so much! I’m glad mine is shaping up to the other entries you’ve read thus far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! It really touches me to know that you enjoyed the article, especially so with the bits about the Foundation knowing when to quit- as a matter of fact, this originally had much more of a downer ending that implied they just looped through this sequence of events forever 3125-style, but I decided that wasn’t the message I wanted to convey and I’m glad the current one worked out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this was a really fun read. I'm a sucker for all things antimemetic, and I love the crosslinks to qntm's series of tales. it's also refreshing to read a piece where for once, the foundation understands they need to leave well enough alone. for such smart people, they sure don't seem to know when to quit sometimes. and I like that the MTF survives — seems to be happening more and more lately, but it's still always a pleasant surprise. and gives some credence to the theory that these entities don't necessarily wish us any harm. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha, I’m happy you caught that bit. The original idea for this was actually something along the lines of “haha what if there was a SCP-3000 for mnestics wouldn’t that be wacky”, but then I sat down and actually thought out what that entailed, and this was the result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to hear I pulled that off, I was a little worried about suspension of disbelief in regards to that part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this one a lot - haven't read all of the SCP-5000 entries but this is a favorite so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic, it's a great use of the format, and I think it fits really well as an -000, and it kind of seems like the counter-entity to 3000, which I really like. The writing is really nice, it's interesting enough to make you want to keep reading, and it leaves you wanting more while providing a pretty satisfying ending. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I started off thinking the whole "mnestics' main component makes you forget stuff in the article" was maybe a little too cute, but you justified it by the end. This is pretty fun. :)

item.title: Re: My Thoughts
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much for your kind words! I’m so glad you enjoyed the article and it’s great to hear that I managed to exemplify the theme well, this really means a lot to me.

item.title: My Thoughts
item.content:encoded: First of all, insane SCP. Of the few entries I’ve read so far, yours is definitely one of the best in my opinion. It perfectly catches the theme of mystery while still allowing the reader to actually know about the SCP. Well done. +1 vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If anyone out there still remembers, read this while you can.

This is cutting it quite close to the deadline, but here’s my 5K entry. I drew slight inspiration from Eskobar’s SCP-2998 for the basic idea behind the formatting. Thanks so much to Thegeniusyoshi for critiquing my draft over Discord!

Image sources, do not sue the sword:
Mnestics
Supply Site
SCP-5000-3

My preferred slots, obviously excluding 5000: 5997 > 5900 > 5333 > 5995 > 5599
